actual_coverage.reasoning: Both focus on multi-year durable growth drivers and the bridge to EPS/ROIC, which would elicit a similar strategic answer; the actual also asks for a pre-pandemic comparison not explicitly in the prediction.
Predicted asks to quantify regional headwinds by quarter and salvage assumptions, aligning with the actual's interest in Q2/Q3 pressure and confidence in the back-half/yield cadence.
Both target Q3 Med dynamics: how much damage was done, how much has rebounded/shifted, and the assumptions embedded in guidance.
Focus is on booking trajectory and whether softness is concentrated close-in vs farther out; predicted asks lead-time distribution and concentration, which addresses the core uncertainty.
Directly matches Perfect Day Mexico/Costa Maya and Royal Beach Club ramp, yield uplift and payback; management would provide the same ramp/structural yield commentary.
Actual asks about construction pause/resumption; prediction addresses destination economics and ramp but not construction status.
Actual asks whether current headwinds reverse in 2027; prediction explicitly asks what portion of yield is sustainable into 2027 versus transitory.
Both seek to decompose yield into like-for-like vs new hardware/destinations; the actual adds an explicit Europe-yield question not directly in the prediction.
Asks about actions given higher fuel and whether cost savings are timing vs structural; prediction covers hedging framework and operational mitigants beyond hedging, leading to a similar response.
Fuel-hedging approach in a high fuel environment is directly matched; the capacity/NCCx-fuel threshold piece is adjacent but not explicitly in the prediction.
Both focus on loyalty/cardholder initiatives' impact on booking/spend and repeat behavior; would drive a similar quantification of net yield lift.
Actual targets NA airfare pressure on demand; prediction mentions air/flight impacts in a geopolitical context, which is a different driver and ask.

missed_actual_themes: Status of Mexico private-destination construction pause/resumption
Impact of higher North American airfares on consumer behavior and demand
overpredicted_themes: Detailed AI/digital attribution and onboard pre-booking metrics
Ship orderbook/capacity deployment mix and new-ship premiums
summary: Coverage was strong on the core themes management addressed: yield decomposition and cadence amid Mediterranean headwinds, booking trajectory/visibility, private-destination ramp (especially Perfect Day Mexico), fuel-hedging approach and operational mitigants, and loyalty/cardholder impact on yields. Two actuals were not covered: an operational construction-status update for Mexico and the effect of higher North American airfares. Precision was moderate: many predictions focused on capital returns, JVs, financing, AI attribution, and ship order cadence, which did not appear in the actual Q&A.
